entries.d: Can make you very ill or kill you if you eat or touch it
entries.s: Don't touch that strange purple plant in the forest — it might be _____ and could make you very sick.

entries.d: Ready to attack or fight
entries.s: The big brown dog looked very _____, so we slowly walked away without looking it in the eyes.

entries.d: Can hurt or harm you
entries.s: Swimming in that fast flowing river is very _____ because the current is strong and can pull you under.

entries.d: Can kill you
entries.s: The bite of some tropical snakes is _____ and can kill a human in less than thirty minutes without medicine.

entries.d: Looking beautiful and stylish
entries.s: The graceful white swan on the quiet lake looked very _____ as it glided smoothly over the water.

entries.d: Extremely beautiful or impressive
entries.s: The view from the top of the mountain was so _____ that everyone just stood there silent with their mouths open in amazement.

entries.d: Pretty and attractive, often small
entries.s: The baby bunny rabbits in the pet shop are so small, fluffy and _____ — I just want to say 'awww!' and hug them.

entries.d: Covered with soft, thick hair
entries.s: My long-haired Persian cat is so _____ all over her body that she looks like a big ball of soft fur on the sofa.

entries.d: Soft and nice to hold close
entries.s: She got a soft _____ teddy bear for her birthday that she hugs and sleeps with every single night.

entries.d: A young bear, lion or wolf
entries.s: The little polar bear _____ stayed very close to its big mother on the ice and copied everything she did.

entries.d: Something you say to wish someone well before a test or event
entries.s: Your big maths exam is tomorrow morning? _____! I really hope you do well and pass.

entries.d: A large white animal that lives in cold places near the Arctic
entries.s: The huge white _____ walked slowly across the thick snow and icy surface looking for seals to hunt.

entries.d: Very cute and easy to love
entries.s: The new tiny puppy in our family is so _____ — everyone who sees him says 'Oh, how sweet!'

entries.d: When an animal uses its teeth to hurt you
entries.s: She had a red itchy mark on her arm from a mosquito _____ last night when we were outside.

entries.d: To make something happen, usually something bad
entries.s: The heavy rain for three days _____ a big dangerous flood in the small village by the river.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with CAUSE?

entries.d: A substance that can make you very ill or kill you
entries.s: Some tropical frogs have strong _____ on their skin to protect them from enemies that try to eat them.

entries.d: A very dangerous disease that animals can give to humans by biting
entries.s: If a wild angry squirrel bites you, it might have _____ — a very dangerous disease you can get from animals.

entries.d: A sea animal with smooth fur that lives in cold water
entries.s: We saw a group of ten grey _____ with flippers lying together on the wet rocks by the cold blue sea.

entries.d: A large white bird with a long neck that lives on water
entries.s: There were two beautiful long-necked white _____ swimming gracefully on the quiet lake with their babies behind them.

entries.d: To use your teeth to cut into something
entries.s: The hungry dog jumped up and tried to _____ a big piece of the sausage out of my hand.

entries.d: A small animal with four legs, a long tail and cold blood
entries.s: A tiny bright green _____ with scales was sitting on the warm stone in the hot sun, not moving at all.

entries.d: To run after someone or something to make them leave
entries.s: The farmer's big brown dog _____ the hungry fox from the chicken garden by barking loudly at it.

entries.d: To say you are not happy about something
entries.s: Some people in our class always _____ loudly about the school food being boring and tasteless every day.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with COMPLAIN?

entries.d: Damage to a part of your body
entries.s: She couldn't play the big football match because of a bad knee _____ she got from falling last week.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with INJURY?

entries.d: To move something up from a lower place
entries.s: Can you please help me _____ this very heavy cardboard box full of books up onto the high top shelf?

entries.d: To move something or someone towards the ground
entries.s: The strong crashing waves on the beach _____ the little swimmer under the water for a few scary seconds.

entries.d: To say yes to something or to take what is given
entries.s: I really hope she will kindly _____ my written apology and forgive me for what I said yesterday.

entries.d: Right now, without waiting
entries.s: When the loud fire alarm rang in the hallway, every student left the building _____ without any delay.

entries.g: (jdn.) von etw. abraten, (jdn.) vor etw. warnen
entries.d: To tell someone not to do something
entries.s: The lifeguard _____ us strongly against swimming in the deep cold water of the lake because of the strong currents.

entries.d: To lose blood from your body
entries.s: His finger started to _____ red blood after he accidentally cut it with a sharp kitchen knife while cooking.

entries.d: When a person or animal stops living
entries.s: The sudden _____ of our beloved old family cat Whiskers last Sunday made the whole family very sad for many weeks.

entries.d: To protect someone or something from attack
entries.s: A fierce mother brown bear will always _____ her little cubs from any predator, even much bigger animals.

entries.d: To think one thing is something else
entries.s: I _____ the white sugar in the bowl for salt and my morning coffee tasted absolutely terrible and gross.

entries.d: The usual or typical amount
entries.s: _____, each student in our class reads about five books per year — some read ten, others only read two.

entries.d: A person who swims under water with special breathing equipment
entries.s: The experienced _____ with her oxygen tank saw many colourful tropical fish near the beautiful coral reef at 20 metres deep.

entries.d: The form or outline of something
entries.s: The new swimming pool in the park was built in the _____ of a five-pointed star — very unusual!

entries.d: To think something is probably true
entries.s: I _____ we should probably leave now for the station if we don't want to be late for the 6pm train to Vienna.

entries.d: To be careful or to look after something
entries.s: Please _____ when you cross that very busy main road — look left and right carefully before stepping out.

entries.d: A person who is hurt or harmed
entries.s: The _____ of the car accident on the motorway was quickly taken to the nearest hospital by ambulance.

entries.d: To share information or talk with others
entries.s: Animals _____ with each other in many different ways — with sounds, body movements, smells, and colours.

entries.d: The people who watch or listen to a show or speech
entries.s: The huge _____ of 1000 people clapped loudly and cheered after the amazing rock concert finished on stage.

entries.d: The natural world around us — air, water, land
entries.s: We should all protect the _____ by using less plastic, recycling paper, and planting more trees.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with ENVIRONMENT?

entries.d: Don't touch!
entries.s: _____ That big chocolate birthday cake on the table is for the party tonight and I don't want anyone touching it!

entries.d: To tell someone about something important
entries.s: Please _____ the teacher by phone or email if you are going to be absent or late to school tomorrow.

entries.d: To put someone in a room or cage and close the door
entries.s: The farmer _____ the chickens inside the safe wooden coop at night to keep them safe from hungry foxes.

entries.d: A person whose job is to work in government and make laws
entries.s: The _____ in the government promised to build a new big park and swimming pool in our small town next year.
